paragraphs.body: This is the story of Tank Dell. It all started when he was born, he was given the name Nathaneil Dell as a baby, Tank was given the name from his mom Porsche Dell. She was a great striving person and good mother, Dell was born on October 29, 1999, Dell was an energetic baby and full of energy and always wanted to pick up a football. He just knew he wanted to be a football player. Dell was given the name Tank because of his mother, she started calling him Tank because his head was big and oversized for him to be a baby. Dell’s people got used to saying Tank and other friends of his started to say Tank as well. Dell said he was never a big fan of the name Nathaneil either, so he says it just clicked and he has been going with Tank for the rest of his life. Dell is from Dayton Beach Florida he attended the high school called Mainland High school. During Dell’s time there he was an undersized quick, fast hands, fast feet wide receiver. He had all the tangibles, but he was just undersized and that really hurt him in some aeras.

annotations.text: He had all the tangibles, but he was just undersized and that really hurt him in some aeras.
annotations.comment: Because this is the last sentence in your summary paragraph, it feels like the "pointing" sentence (the "art" of summary). Is this idea important for your own essay?

annotations.text: This is the story of Tank Dell. It

paragraphs.body: Tank was a hot topic in Florida at the time, but he wasn’t heavily recruited. He had some schools, but they were small, and some were HBCU’s. So, Tank had one option to go to one of those schools and Tank decided to go to Alabama A&M. During his time at Alabama A&M he did some unbelievable stuff, as a freshman he racked up some of the most impressive stats as a freshman and was one of the best wide receivers in the swac conference. Tank had led the team with 29 receptions for 428 yards and three touchdowns over eight games and those stats as freshman are impressive and Tank knew he was bigger than the level he was at so the only way he was going to achieve his goal is bye going to a smaller school to get to a bigger school. So, Tank had to prove himself again by going to the JUCO level, Tank decided to go to JUCO called Independence College, located in Kansas. During Tank’s time at the Juco, he spent hours grinding and getting better and crafting his route running. Tank trained with this wide receiver coach that goes by the name Margin Hooks in Dallas Texas. Me and Tank Dell met then, and we were training together. I used to just try and mimic everything he does with his feet and how he just moves in general, his playing style is so similar to my playing style. During Tanks time at the JUCO Independence College, as a sophomore Tank achieved 788 rec yards and 52 catches and 8 TDs, those stats as a sophomore are pretty good and gave him the opportunity to get recruited again during that time Tank was one of the best wide receiver to come out of the Juco. Tank had a big dream to stay close to home or go back to Florida and play at home, but then once the Houston Cougars offered him a scholarship Tank took a visit there and he knew that this was the place for him. The Cougar nation loved him as well.

annotations.text: Me and Tank Dell met then, and we were training together. I used to just try and mimic everything he does with his feet and how he just moves in general, his playing style is so similar to my playing style.

paragraphs.body: Tank Dell went off to play at the University of Houston and entering his junior year at this university Tank tells the press “I'm here now this what I been working for God didn't put me here for no reason we here”. Tank had so many more opportunities at the University of Houston with the NFL and all the exposure playing at the power 5 level. Tank’s junior year he had a pretty good season. He was a top 25 wide receiver and in the nation, he led college football in catches and touchdowns. Going into the mid-season Tank was getting very popular and he was winning awards. He was almost nominated for the Biletnikoff award as a junior. Tank Dell’s senior year he was one of the best wide receivers in college football and he was projected to be in the 1st round of the NFL draft this season. So, Tank would have to prove himself even harder to maintain this image and to prove that he can be an undersized wide receiver and ball out in the NFL. Tank went crazy his senior year by leading the nation in 17 touchdowns and having 1,837 receiving yards leading the nation as well as having 109 receptions. Tank did the impossible and standing at 5’10 and weighing 155 pounds. Houston didn't make the playoffs, and they had a bowl game in my home town so I had to go see Tank Dell play. So, I went to his last college football game, and he had a great game with 3 touchdowns and 5 catches for 207 yards. After the game I got to meet up with Tank Dell and get a picture with him and he gave me one of his last pair of Houston gloves.

annotations.text: Tank did the impossible and standing at 5’10 and weighing 155 pounds.
annotations.comment: This sentence about Tank's size is a repetition of your comment about size in your first paragraph. There seems to be some "heat" and "energy" here. Let's talk.
Again, this essay is still in summary mode. Where is your voice? Where is your essay? Why is Tank's story so important to you? Let's explore.

paragraphs.body: After that game Tank Dell was invited to play in the Reese's senior bowl game. He got to play in the game, and he went off to go the NFL combine. During the NFL combine he had an outstanding camp. He ran a 4.50 40-yard dash, that was really fast for a WR, but his draft stock really did drop a little because of him being an undersized wide receiver he should be running faster, but Tank didn't let that defeat him. A couple weeks after the combine Tank was getting ready for draft day and it was almost time, and he finally got the call, and it was from the Houston Texans and they drafted him in the 1st round 63rd pick and that was a very great experience for Tank . Tank accomplish so many obstacles to get to the point where he is right now he finally did it.

annotations.text: Tank accomplish so many obstacles to get to the point where he is right now he finally did it.
